title: apera spica-venti subsp. intermedia
intro: Apera spica-venti subsp. intermedia, also known as the intermediate hair grass or meadow millet, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Apera spica-venti subsp. intermedia has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Apera spica-venti subsp. intermedia include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and aid in wound healing. Apera spica-venti subsp. intermedia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to leverage its medicinal properties.
